title: Bridge- and route-aware sampling

queries: graph retrieval route aware sampling bridge table evidence graph llm
graph rag route aware evidence selection bridge table retention structured reasoning

papers.title: G-Retriever: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Graph Question Answering
papers.claim: A single compressed graph prompt can lose structural fidelity, so selecting the right evidence subgraph is often more important than adding more prompt-side decoration.
papers.relevance: Supports moving from repeated alignment-loss failures to a graph-evidence selection change.

papers.title: Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Survey
papers.claim: Graph indexing, route-aware retrieval, and evidence organization are primary design axes for graph-enhanced generation.
papers.relevance: Supports a route- and bridge-aware sampling bundle as the next cross-task branch after loss-family saturation.

papers.title: Can we Soft Prompt LLMs for Graph Learning Tasks?
papers.claim: Prompt-side graph quality depends on preserving useful structure before projection into the LLM prompt space.
papers.relevance: Supports treating better evidence exposure, not more alignment penalties, as the next likely bottleneck.

evidence.why_now: Uniform neighbor pruning already failed in EXP045, but two successive route-aware conservative losses also failed with the same representative-task pattern. The next stronger paper-backed move is therefore not another near-duplicate loss tweak, but a more selective prompt-side evidence exposure change inside the sampling layer.

hypothesis: The current prompt-side evidence path is still exposing structurally weak or noisy neighborhoods because recursive sampling serializes whatever first-hop nodes survive the loader without prioritizing bridge-mediated or stable route evidence. A bridge- and route-aware sampler that deterministically preserves high-value route continuations inside the already loaded batch subgraph should improve cross-task stability more safely than adding another alignment loss.
notes: This candidate shifts Stage 3 from constraint-aware loss to constraint-aware sampling.
Intended implementation hook: replace purely type-avoidant recursive prompt sampling with deterministic bridge- and route-aware retention inside the existing batch subgraph.
Do not relaunch uniform neighbor pruning; this candidate must preserve evidence quality rather than just shrink prompt volume.
Launched on 2026-05-07 after syncing the updated main.py/model.py sampling patch plus generated wrappers and launcher to the remote repo and /tmp.
Completed on 2026-05-07 with global verdict: failed.

All three representative tasks regressed versus the refreshed 512-step strict baselines, so treat this first constraint-aware sampling branch as scientific failure rather than an implementation issue.
